code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test12-JUL-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Low serum glucose and low total CO₂ may indicate metabolic disturbances. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Findings are consistent with possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology; clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
